title: Static, dataless search/browse UI (faceted; metadata-only)
context: The catalog's searchability is one of its core value propositions. The UI must be a static, client-side site that indexes catalog metadata only — no backend data store, no tracking, no patient data. The search index is pre-built over the catalog JSON. It must be faceted by cancer type, repository, access tier, license, and entryType (the typed field distinguishing patient-cohort from cell-model-collection from population-statistics from molecular-dataset). REGISTERED-OPEN and NON-COMMERCIAL (if any) records must carry a prominent access badge. CONTROLLED records must never appear (the catalog has none). Depends on schema-001 (for the catalog record shape) and the M1 indexing tasks (for a corpus to build the UI over).
objective: Build a static, client-side search/browse site over the catalog JSON records, faceted by cancer type, repository, access tier, license, and entryType, with prominent access badges for REGISTERED-OPEN and NON-COMMERCIAL records, no backend, no tracking, and no patient data exposure.
acceptanceCriteria: A static, client-side search/browse site is built over the catalog JSON records; no backend server, no database, no user-tracking, no cookies beyond functional state.
Search is faceted by: cancer type (using OncoTree/NCIt codes and free-text name), hosting repository, access tier (OPEN | OPEN-AGGREGATE | REGISTERED-OPEN), license, and entryType.

REGISTERED-OPEN records carry a prominent 'Registration required' badge; NON-COMMERCIAL records (if admitted per policy-nc-034) carry a prominent 'Non-commercial use only' badge; CONTROLLED records never appear.
The UI exposes only cohort-level metadata (no per-patient data, no row-level data, no patient identifiers, no clinical outcomes beyond aggregate counts).
The site builds from the catalog JSON in the repository without a network call to any external data source at runtime; catalog JSON is the single source of truth.
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint green; code is MIT-licensed; DCO signed-off.
output: A committed static search/browse UI (MIT) over the catalog JSON, faceted by cancer type/repository/access tier/license/entryType, with prominent access badges for REGISTERED-OPEN and NON-COMMERCIAL records, and no patient data or tracking.
